description: PI research taste profile. Populated over time by the ideation agent based on user selections and explicit preferences. Read by r01-ideation and r01-novelty-checker to bias idea generation toward the PI's preferred research directions. Do not delete entries — append and update only.

hypothesis_framing.description: Preferred hypothesis types. Examples: 'mechanistic' (test a causal pathway), 'implementation' (deploy and evaluate), 'evaluation' (measure outcomes), 'comparative-effectiveness' (head-to-head comparison). Populated after the PI selects ideas across 2+ projects.

schema.type: string enum: mechanistic | implementation | evaluation | comparative-effectiveness | mixed-methods

methodological_tendency.description: Study design preferences inferred from idea selections and explicit feedback. preferred_designs: designs the PI gravitates toward. avoided_designs: designs the PI consistently rejects.

schema.evidence: string — which project selections or feedback support this preference

risk_appetite.description: How willing the PI is to propose high-risk aims. null = not yet calibrated. Scale: 'conservative' (safe, incremental), 'moderate' (balanced risk/reward), 'aggressive' (high-risk-high-reward, tolerates novel_with_differentiation novelty verdicts). Inferred from selection patterns — does the PI pick the safe option or the bold one?

schema.level: string enum: conservative | moderate | aggressive | null
schema.evidence_entry: string — project name + selection context supporting this calibration

topic_preferences.description: Research themes the PI gravitates toward or avoids. Populated from selection patterns and explicit statements. Examples of themes: 'alert fatigue', 'shared decision-making', 'health equity', 'wearable monitoring', 'NLP on clinical notes', 'patient self-management'.

schema.evidence: string — which selections or feedback support this

scope_preference.description: Structural preferences for proposal architecture. preferred_aim_count: 2-4 (null = no preference). aim_independence: 'sequential' (aims build on each other), 'parallel' (aims can proceed independently), 'mixed' (some sequential, some parallel), null = no preference. Used by novelty checker to flag branches that don't match.

schema.aim_independence: string enum: sequential | parallel | mixed | null

selection_history_schema.branches_presented: int — number of branches offered to user
selection_history_schema.selected_branch_id: string — id of the branch the user chose
rejected_branch_ids: string — ids of branches not chosen

rejection_reasons.description: Per-branch rejection reasons if user provided them, otherwise null

selection_history_schema.modifications_requested: string or null — changes the user requested before confirming selection

auto_update_rules.description: Rules for when the ideation agent should auto-update preference fields based on selection_history patterns.

rules.trigger: 3+ selections show consistent pattern in methodological approach
rules.notification: Flag to user: 'I noticed you consistently prefer X — I've added this to your ideation preferences.'

rules.trigger: 3+ selections show consistent risk level choice
rules.notification: Flag to user: 'Based on your selections, your risk appetite appears to be X.'

rules.trigger: 3+ rejections cite similar theme or approach
rules.notification: Flag to user: 'I noticed you consistently avoid X — I've added this to your avoided themes.'
